_sources.discrepancies: Michigan enacted Proposal 2 (2022) which added same-day registration, 9 days of early voting, and other provisions. NCSL categorizes Michigan as 'photo ID requested' — voters without ID sign an affidavit. The Michigan SoS confirms photo ID is requested but not strictly required: voters may sign a statement of identity instead. Michigan's 2026 primary date is set by statute as the first Tuesday after the second Monday in August.

earlyVoting.notes: Michigan requires 9 days of early voting (Proposal 2, 2022). Exact locations set by local clerks. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
Michigan requires at least 9 days of early voting before Election Day. Locations and hours set by local clerks. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.

Michigan requires at least 9 days of early in-person voting, ending the Sunday before Election Day. Locations set by local clerks. Same-day registration available through Election Day at your local clerk's office. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.

acceptedIds: Student ID from a Michigan college or university
votingRules.expirationRule: Photo ID should not be expired. If a voter does not have an acceptable photo ID, they may sign a statement of identity (affidavit) and their ballot will be counted. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
votingRules.impedimentDeclaration: Voters without photo ID may sign an affidavit (statement of identity) and vote a regular ballot — no provisional ballot required.
votingRules.phonesAtPollsDetail: Michigan does not prohibit phones at polling places. Voters may not photograph their ballot in a way that reveals how they voted. No electioneering within 100 feet of the polling place.
additionalRules: Open primary: voters may vote in either party's primary without registering with a party.
No runoffs in Michigan — the candidate with the most votes wins.
All registered voters automatically receive an absentee ballot application before each election.
Same-day voter registration available through Election Day at your local clerk's office.

eligibility: Any registered voter may request an absentee ballot without providing a reason
voteByMail.applicationDeadline: 5 p.m. on the Friday before the election for mailed applications; applications in person at the clerk's office accepted through 4 p.m. on Election Day
voteByMail.returnDeadline: Must be received by the local clerk by 8 p.m. on Election Day
voteByMail.returnDeadlinePlain: Must arrive at the local clerk by poll closing (8 p.m.) on Election Day. Postmarks do not count.

primaryParticipation.ruleExplanationEn: Michigan has an open primary (Mich. Comp. Laws §168.613a). Any registered voter may vote in either party's primary. Voters may only vote in one party's primary per election.
primaryParticipation.ruleExplanationEs: Michigan tiene una primaria abierta (Mich. Comp. Laws §168.613a). Cualquier votante registrado puede votar en la primaria de cualquier partido. Los votantes solo pueden votar en la primaria de un partido por elección.
